skill.name: yao-meta-skill
skill.description: Create, refactor, evaluate, and package agent skills from workflows, prompts, transcripts, docs, or notes. Use for skill creation, reusable workflow packaging, skill improvement, evals, and team-ready distribution.
skill.summary: Create, refactor, evaluate, and package agent skills from workflows, prompts, transcripts, docs, or ...
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Legitimate skill-authoring meta-tool. Static analyzer's 12,748 raw findings are dominated by (a) markdown backticks across 619 docs and preview HTML/JSON files, (b) URL strings inside preview output JSON/HTML artifacts, and (c) 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' matches inside test-data JSON (data, not code). All actual code paths are well-bounded: subprocess calls use argv lists with sys.executable (no shell=True) and a hardcoded allowlist in apply_adaptation.py (make <target> or python3 scripts/tests/*.py); check_update.py is HTTPS-only and pinned to the project's own raw.githubusercontent.com host; os.environ reads are limited to documented YAO_* keys and GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES for git apply. No exfiltration, no obfuscation, no dangerous combinations observed.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess invocation with argv list (no shell=True)
medium_findings.description: scripts/yao_cli_runtime.py:31 uses subprocess.run([sys.executable, script_path(name), *args]) and scripts/apply_adaptation.py:146 calls subprocess.run(parts) with an allowlisted command. No user-controlled string is ever passed to a shell, and apply_adaptation.py rejects any command outside {make <safe-target>, python3 scripts/tests/*.py, python3 tests/*.py}. Confidence that this is benign: 0.9. False positive: the pattern is well-bounded.

medium_findings.title: Outbound HTTPS call to GitHub raw host
medium_findings.description: scripts/check_update.py:54 fetches the project's own VERSION and manifest.json from raw.githubusercontent.com for an opt-in self-update check. URL is hardcoded and validate_update_url() rejects anything that is not https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yaojingang/yao-meta-skill/ unless --allow-custom-update-url is set. Confidence benign: 0.95. False positive in this context.

low_findings.title: Reads YAO_* environment variables and GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES
low_findings.description: scripts/check_update.py reads YAO_UPDATE_VERSION_URL, YAO_UPDATE_MANIFEST_URL, YAO_ALLOW_CUSTOM_UPDATE_URL; scripts/apply_adaptation.py and yao_cli_create_commands.py set GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES to bound git apply operations to the skill directory. All keys are documented in --help text and used only for the local self-update and patch-apply flows. Confidence benign: 0.95.

low_findings.title: Filesystem reads/writes confined to skill working directory
low_findings.description: The skill reads and writes files under its own root (manifest.json, VERSION, reports/, .yao/, scripts/, tests/) to scaffold, validate, lint, package, and report on skills. No path traversal or writes outside the skill's own scope were observed. Confidence benign: 0.95.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bulk 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' matches in test/report JSON
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged thousands of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' hits. The vast majority of these are inside test fixture JSON, preview report HTML/JSON, and skill-IR artifacts under .previews/ and reports/. These are data records (skill metadata, eval scores, generated HTML), not cryptographic operations. Confidence the underlying code is safe: 0.9.

content.user_title: Author production-grade agent skills with a disciplined engineering workflow
content.value_statement: Stop hand-rolling SKILL.md files in isolation. yao-meta-skill gives you a complete authoring pipeline: scaffold, lint, govern, package, and ship agent skills that are routed correctly, evaluated against real outputs, and ready for team distribution.
actual_capabilities: Scaffold new agent skills from a description, transcript, workflow, or set of notes, producing SKILL.md, agents/interface.yaml, and a justified folder layout.
Refactor existing skills into a lean SKILL.md plus references/, scripts/, and evidence/ subfolders following the Skill OS 2.0 layout.
Run validation, lint, and governance checks on any skill directory with a single command.
Compile skills into a portable package, verify the package, and produce a trust report.
Run output evaluation and blind-judge evals against real provider calls to score skill output quality.
Generate Review Studio HTML/JSON reports for human review of skill output decisions.
Check for skill updates from the project's GitHub release and surface a non-blocking upgrade hint.
limitations: This is an authoring and packaging tool, not a runtime skill executor; it produces and validates skills, it does not invoke them against a live agent.

Output evaluation makes real provider HTTP calls and requires the user to supply provider credentials and consent; offline mode is supported but produces 'missing evidence' labels rather than fabricated scores.
Skill IR, Skill Atlas, and Review Studio artifacts are generated as local files under the skill directory; the skill does not push to a remote registry on its own.

use_cases.title: Build a new skill from a workflow description
use_cases.description: Capture a recurring job, scaffold the SKILL.md, references, and scripts, then validate before committing.

use_cases.title: Package an existing skill for team distribution
use_cases.description: Compile, verify, run governance and trust checks, and produce a shareable package plus a trust report.

use_cases.title: Run an output-quality evaluation before shipping
use_cases.description: Trigger output eval and blind-judge evals on a candidate skill, then import reviewer decisions to update the scorecard.

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold a new skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold a new skill called geo-content-brief that turns a topic and target keywords into a structured content brief. Mode: production. Include an output contract, a system model, and a small example. Do not include telemetry or review-studio gates yet.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and lint an existing skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the skill under ./skills/geo-content-brief with --require-manifest and show me a summary of any governance failures.

prompt_templates.title: Package and verify
prompt_templates.prompt: Compile and package the skill under ./skills/geo-content-brief, then run package-verify and trust. Report the result as JSON.

prompt_templates.title: Run an output evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run output-eval on ./skills/geo-content-brief using the prompts in evals/prompts.jsonl. Use the configured provider. If provider credentials are missing, mark metrics as 'missing evidence' rather than fabricate them.

output: evidence: 'missing evidence' for any metric the provider could not supply

best_practices: Start from the user's outcome before structure: capture job, output, exclusions, constraints, and standards first, then choose the lightest reliable process.
Keep SKILL.md lean: put guidance in references/, logic in scripts/, and evidence in reports/. Only add folders and gates you have earned.
Always run validate, lint, and governance (with --require-manifest) before packaging a skill for distribution.
Mark unavailable telemetry, approvals, metrics, or benchmarks as 'missing evidence' rather than fabricating values.

For governed or release-critical skills, run the full Skill OS 2.0 gate set (Skill IR, target compiler, output eval, Skill Atlas, conformance, trust, registry/package/install, upgrade, drift, waiver, Review Studio) before release.
anti_patterns: Creating a skill for a one-off task; if there is no reusable output contract and no repeated use, do not create a skill.
Stuffing guidance, scripts, and evidence all into SKILL.md; this breaks routing and review.
Fabricating output eval scores when the provider call failed or credentials are missing; always mark 'missing evidence' instead.
Bypassing --require-manifest during package-verify to ship a skill that lacks owner, review cadence, or rollback boundary.
Allowing custom HTTPS update URLs without explicit opt-in; the default self-update source must stay pinned to the project's own GitHub host.

faq.question: What does yao-meta-skill actually do?
faq.answer: It is a meta-tool for creating, refactoring, validating, evaluating, and packaging agent skills. It produces SKILL.md, scripts, references, and reports, and verifies that the resulting package meets the Skill OS 2.0 governance and trust gates.

faq.question: Does this skill run other skills for me?
faq.answer: No. It is an authoring and packaging tool. It does not invoke a candidate skill against a live agent; it only builds, validates, and evaluates skill packages.

faq.question: Does it make network calls?
faq.answer: Only an opt-in self-update check to raw.githubusercontent.com/yaojingang/yao-meta-skill/ over HTTPS, and explicit provider calls you trigger via output-eval. Custom update URLs require --allow-custom-update-url.

faq.question: What happens if output-eval cannot reach the provider?
faq.answer: Missing metrics are recorded as 'missing evidence' in reports/output_quality_scorecard.md. The skill never fabricates evaluation scores.

faq.question: Is it safe to run yao package-verify on a skill directory?
faq.answer: Yes. package-verify reads the skill directory, validates manifest, governance, and trust, and writes a verification report. It does not apply patches or run untrusted code on its own; the separate apply-adaptation command uses a strict allowlist (make targets and python3 scripts/tests/*.py) and is only run when you explicitly ask for it.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill declares compatibility with claude, codex, and claude-code in its manifest.
